routes.name: Trans-Pacific (North)
routes.notes: World's highest-value trade lane. Connects China's manufacturing heartland to the US consumer market.

routes.name: Asia–Europe (Suez Canal Route)
routes.notes: Critical artery connecting Asian manufacturing to European markets. Disrupted by Houthi attacks on Red Sea shipping since 2023, forcing rerouting around Cape of Good Hope.

routes.name: Transatlantic Route
routes.notes: Historic trade route underpinning the transatlantic economic relationship between the US and EU.

routes.name: Persian Gulf Oil Route (East)
routes.notes: ~20% of global oil supply transits the Strait of Hormuz. Iran's ability to close the Strait is a constant geopolitical leverage point.

routes.name: Persian Gulf Oil Route (West)
routes.notes: Passes through Bab el-Mandeb and Suez Canal. Houthi attacks in Red Sea 2023-present have significantly disrupted this route.

routes.name: Intra-Asia Route
routes.notes: Backbone of Asian regional supply chains. Passes through or near South China Sea territorial disputes.

routes.name: Cape of Good Hope Bypass
routes.notes: Usage surged 50%+ after 2023 Houthi attacks on Red Sea shipping. Adds 10-14 days to Asia-Europe transit, significantly increasing shipping costs.

routes.name: Malacca–India Route
routes.notes: Key link for India's energy imports from the Gulf and trade with Southeast Asia.

chokepoints.name: Strait of Malacca
chokepoints.summary: World's busiest shipping strait connecting the Pacific and Indian Oceans. One-third of global trade passes through annually. Piracy historically present but suppressed by regional patrols.
chokepoints.currentThreat: Piracy at low levels. China's ability to blockade Malacca is a core strategic anxiety for Beijing ('Malacca Dilemma').

chokepoints.name: Strait of Hormuz
chokepoints.summary: The only sea route from the Persian Gulf to the open ocean. ~20% of global oil supply and ~25% of LNG trade passes through. Iran controls the northern shore and repeatedly threatens closure.
chokepoints.currentThreat: Iran has mined tankers, seized vessels, and threatened closure multiple times. Iranian proxy (Houthi) attacks in adjacent Red Sea have elevated regional maritime risk.

chokepoints.name: Suez Canal
chokepoints.summary: Connects the Mediterranean and Red Seas, providing the shortest sea route between Europe and Asia. ~12% of global trade transits the canal, including 30% of container traffic.
chokepoints.currentThreat: Ever Given grounding (2021) demonstrated fragility. Houthi attacks in the Red Sea have reduced canal traffic ~40% since late 2023 as ships reroute around Africa.

chokepoints.name: Bab el-Mandeb
chokepoints.summary: Narrow strait between Yemen and the Horn of Africa connecting the Red Sea to the Gulf of Aden. Gateway to the Suez Canal for Asia-Europe shipping. Only 29km wide at its narrowest point.
chokepoints.currentThreat: ACTIVE THREAT: Houthi missile and drone attacks on commercial vessels since October 2023. Major shipping companies rerouting around Cape of Good Hope. US-UK conducting strikes on Houthi launch sites.

chokepoints.name: Strait of Gibraltar
chokepoints.summary: Gateway between the Atlantic Ocean and the Mediterranean Sea. Controlled by UK (Gibraltar) and Spain. Critical for European energy imports from North Africa and traffic between Atlantic and Mediterranean.
chokepoints.currentThreat: Low threat. UK-Spain diplomatic friction over Gibraltar sovereignty. Strategic importance to NATO for Mediterranean access.

chokepoints.name: Turkish Straits (Bosphorus)
chokepoints.summary: The Bosphorus and Dardanelles connect the Black Sea to the Mediterranean. Turkey controls access under the 1936 Montreux Convention. Critical for Russian Black Sea Fleet and Ukrainian grain exports.
chokepoints.currentThreat: Turkey invoked Montreux Convention to block warship passage following Russia's 2022 Ukraine invasion. Russia's Black Sea Fleet significantly degraded by Ukrainian drone/missile attacks.

chokepoints.name: Taiwan Strait
chokepoints.summary: ~180km wide strait between Taiwan and mainland China. Approximately 88 vessels transit daily, carrying ~25% of global container traffic. Also home to TSMC's chip fabs — the world's most critical semiconductor supply.
chokepoints.currentThreat: Any Chinese military action against Taiwan would likely trigger US military response and close this strait, causing catastrophic global supply chain disruption. China regularly conducts military exercises simulating blockade.

chokepoints.name: South China Sea
chokepoints.summary: The South China Sea carries approximately one-third of global maritime trade, worth $3-5 trillion annually. China's expanding control of artificial islands and military installations threatens freedom of navigation.
chokepoints.currentThreat: China's coast guard regularly confronts Philippine and Vietnamese vessels. US freedom of navigation operations ongoing. Risk of miscalculation between Chinese and US/allied naval forces increasing.
